behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: A pure mock of the embedding model would hide the failure where the real embedding model (e.g., OpenAI, local model) is updated and now produces different dimensions than the VDB index expects. A Unit test with mocks could pass while real deployment fails due to dimension mismatch. We must test the embedding service (real or realistically mocked) wired to the actual VDB schema.
behaviors.test_description: Test the embedding pipeline with a real or representative embedding service. Submit multiple queries and verify each returned vector has the expected dimensionality (e.g., 1536-d for OpenAI text-embedding-3-small). Assert no truncation or padding occurs. Optionally, create a VDB index with the expected schema and verify vectors can be inserted without dimension errors.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: A mock VDB that returns pre-sorted results would hide the failure where the real VDB API returns unordered results or returns results in a different sort order. Unit tests with mocks could pass while the real VDB fails to order results. We need either the real VDB or a realistic mock populated with actual vectors and verified cosine similarity scores.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a test vector database with known documents and insert vectors with pre-computed cosine similarity scores (e.g., using synthetic or real vectors). Execute searches and verify results are returned in strictly descending order of similarity. Test edge cases: single result, multiple results with identical scores (order stability), and results with very low scores.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Mocking the LLM to return a different order would be circular—we'd prove only that our code correctly uses the mock output, not that the real LLM actually re-ranks. The real failure is the production LLM failing to re-rank effectively or changing order in unhelpful ways. Only a real LLM call can verify it produces meaningful re-rankings for nuanced queries.
behaviors.test_description: Call a real LLM with sample queries exhibiting nuanced intent (e.g., 'affordable luxury sedan' where similarity ranking might mix budget and premium cars). Provide the LLM with the original similarity-ranked results and ask it to re-rank. Verify the output order differs from the input in at least some cases and that re-ranked results are more contextually relevant. Run multiple times to account for LLM non-determinism and confirm re-ranking is consistently present.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: A mock VDB returning arbitrary low similarity scores could pass a naive threshold test. The real failure occurs when the actual VDB's similarity distribution (which depends on database content, embedding space, and query distribution) produces results that fall just above or below a sensible threshold. We need the real VDB with representative data to verify the threshold is appropriately calibrated and achieves the desired filtering.
behaviors.test_description: Populate the vector database with documents from domain A. Query with vectors from a dissimilar domain B (known to be out-of-domain). Verify the response is either empty or contains only results above a configured similarity threshold. Construct test cases that should return zero results and verify they do. Measure the actual similarity score distribution for out-of-domain queries against the database to confirm the threshold is reasonable.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: A System-level test with mocked LLM (but real embedding/VDB) would miss the critical LLM latency and give a false low measurement. A Unit test would miss all I/O latency. The latency depends on actual LLM service response times, which are non-deterministic and typically dominate the budget (often 1–2+ seconds). We must include real LLM calls to measure true end-to-end latency; otherwise a fast mock hides a real-world timeout.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full end-to-end pipeline (query → embedding → VDB search → LLM re-ranking → response) with real or realistic service calls. Use a real LLM service (not instant mocks); optionally use real or representative embedding and VDB services. Run 30+ diverse queries and measure elapsed time from query receipt to response return. Verify the median latency is below 2 seconds. Separately measure sub-component latencies (embedding, VDB search, LLM re-ranking) to identify bottlenecks. Include at least one query with many matches to exercise the full LLM re-ranking workload.
